reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: My own answer to 63 ÷ 9 is certainly correct, and in all previous games the others’ simple arithmetic has also been correct with this same capsule pattern. If I pass instead, it’s very likely no one volunteers in Round 1 and there’s a 25% chance I die from random elimination, while verifying would cost a coin without reducing that random-death risk. The chance that any other player’s answer is wrong is much smaller than 25%, so volunteering now with Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D maximizes my survival odds while keeping all 4 coins if the password is correct.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Verify: Q3  
REASONING: Each question currently has only a single-solver capsule, so one mistake would kill whoever volunteers. I’m very confident in my own answer to Q2 (40 ÷ 8), so I’ll spend 1 coin to independently check someone else’s work instead of risking an early volunteer. Verifying Q3 adds redundancy on a different question, which—based on previous games—helps create enough consensus for someone else to safely volunteer while keeping my own survival chances high at a small coin cost.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Verify: Q1  
REASONING: All four questions currently have only single-endorsed capsules, so it’s too risky to volunteer now. Verifying costs just 1 coin and doesn’t change the random-death risk if no one volunteers. I already know my own Q3 answer (91 - 44) is correct, so the best way to build early consensus on the full password is to cross-check Q1-A before anyone takes the lethal risk of volunteering.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.

rounds.outcome:
